item.title: Nouveau
item.story: Rewind and Refresh. It's a new year, a new site, a new round. The basic concept of this site lies in the idea of "dynamic abstraction." By allowing the site to randomize various elements - the chances of a unique look and feel is increased as are happy accidents and intresting juxtapositions. Also, with the assistance of the talents of Destin Young, we launched lee's new site. Keep your eyes open and ears atune - more to come.

item.title: Least we forget
item.story: I've sat here trying to write something appropriate that sums up my feelings and thoughts on the place of my birth, New Orleans. Nothing seems to work - it's usually a jumble of words much like this is. The piece I did on Katrina is still accesible by clicking on the clock. The one thing I will say in regards to the city that care forgot is - Remember...

item.title: Alan Fletcher
item.story: Twas a sad day in 2006 when Alan Fletcher passed. As writtin by the Design Museum "Synthesising the graphic traditions of Europe and North America to develop a spirited, witty and very personal visual style, ALAN FLETCHER is among the most influential figures in British graphic design as a founder of Fletcher/Forbes/Gill in the 1960s and Pentagram in the 1970s." For a brief overview of his life - check out what the design Museum has put together.

item.title: The Manipulator
item.story: If you're not familiar with the photography of Jill Greenburg and her wonderful portraits of crying babies and screaming apes - I strongly encourage you to visit the manipulator.

item.title: Ingrid Baars
item.story: I came across the amazing photography of Ingrid Baars in a recent book Die Gestalten Verlag published called "All Allure." Her work is provocative, emotional, and painful. Like WK Interact, her work stretches images to help create a bold and unique sense of movement.

item.title: Theme
item.story: I picked up Theme magazine one day a while back. A publication that focuses on the Asian culture ( and subculture ), the design and attention to detail on the magazine are par none. I met both of the editors and founders at a release party in Los Angeles they had for issue 3 at Work Horse. Pick an issue up - ASAP.

item.title: IDEA
item.story: In the most recent issue of IDEA magazine - they asked the question "Which direction is graphic design shifting to?" In answering this question they isolated work from some of the best studios today, of which I have taken a sampling from A to C. Follow the rabbit trail . . . Enjoy. A practice for everyday life, Adrian Shaughnessy, Alon Levin, &&&, Andrew Blauvelt, Angela Lorenz, Anne Burdick, Base, BibliothŹque, Bluesource, Brighten the Corners, Browns, Build, Cartlidge Levene, Catalogtree, Christos Lialios, COMA, Cornel Windlin.

item.title: Poop
item.story: As disgusting as it may look and sound, there is actually a book published by none other than Colors on cacas. It's actually a pretty interesting book and supplies a bounty of well photographed, um, poo. Grab it here.

project.info: These comps were done in collaboration with <a href="http://www.kellerhouse.com//" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Kellerhouse Design,</FONT> the aim was to try and create an iconic and impactful image that would help create an air of intrigue as well as horror.

A process of collaboration that went back and forth for a couple of months - both Aaron and Matt of <a href="http://www.i-manifest.com/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">IMNFST</FONT> were essential in the creation of this shirt. The shirt is printed on the front and inside front, so essentially it's two shirts in one. We also opted to use some glow in the dark pigment for that extra special touch. Yum.

<a href="http://www.lounge72.com/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Lounge 72</FONT> releases an online calendar every year featuring various designers and illustrators - featured here is my piece along with a couple of studies.

The Reaping is a film set to release in March that has had various delays. The premise surrounds a young girl believed to be causing biblical plagues to occur. The art direction and design was done in collaboration with the ever present and wonderful <a href="http://www.evoke.la/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Evoke.</FONT>
Some selects from a recent board completed for <a href="http://www.transistorstudios.com/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Transistor Studios.</FONT>

An unorthodox approach to the usual script-handwriting-with-flowers-and-center-justified type of wedding invitations. This invite was designed as a keepsake; a piece family and friends would frame and cherish, rather than merely sticking it in a drawer to be forgotten.

A commissioned piece for Adobe's online showcase. It needed to "suggest a sense of community," and, in a very abstract way, it does. This piece leaps from detective to a problem-solving maze to a supportive community base. Good times. Good fun. Superb animation and sound design by <a href="http://www.dreamersinkaesthetics.net/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Mitch Paone</FONT> on the piece.
Spend half of a day staring at your favorite typefaces, and devise your own. This was inspired by Paralucent and the ultra thin versions of the always sexy Gotham.

On our way back from Nova Scotia, Karen and I had the pleasure of United airlines delaying our flight for a splendid four hours in Washington. The following sketches are some of the ways I passed the time. A study of characters.

Dentists are more than the lidocaine, drills and nightmarish afternoons we all know so well. Give 'em a chance, and they'll step away from the steadfast Kinko's and hire a creative agency to do the work. Turn a mark on its side, and let's see what we come up with. Tongue-in-cheek? Yes. Unexpected? Absolutely. While these four marks did not make the final cut, they are worthy of showcase nonetheless.

What do you get when you cross old spaghetti westerns, a deep appreciation for Rat Fink, and an understanding of contemporary culture? You get Sputnik Ranch. Here, a fitting identity and collateral for this new boutique.
Not sure if this is the 32nd sketchbook. To be honest I've lost count. A few selected pages are shown here - enjoy.

This piece has been featured before - I still really like it and felt it needed a bit more "show and tell." I like the dual purpose of showcasing Lee's work and reemphasizing his name through out the piece. Lee, by the way, is a bad ass photographer.

Based out of Los Angeles, interactive agency 65 Media needed to redevelop what was essentially a logotype. After a bit of preliminary work, we developed a new take on their logotype, and a mark that complemented it. IAAH collaborated with good friend and co-conspirator Matthew Schiender.

The challenge felt like a daunting one. To establish a logotype for a short series entitled "Batman - Gotham County Lines." Turns out, DC produces many Batman stories and the task of reinventing a icon was not as monolithic as originally thought. Needless to say - it was fun.
Manhattan Wine Company is MWC, and they store wine. This mark is a clever take on the obvious.

This mark was co-developed with the talented <a href="http://www.cobbwerks.com" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Chris Cobb</FONT> for a mutual friend who shall remain anonynomus. Let's just say he's opening his own shop - he's talented - and we feel as though the mark identifies him well in a crowded marketplace.

Book publisher<a href="http://www.die-gestalten.de/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003"> Die Gestalten</FONT> invited IAAH to participate in a book titled, "Play Loud," which is based on the culture of football. The Rorschach illustrations used in the design were aptly fitting after all, many use athletics as a form of therapy, in some form or fashion.

A motion piece for the One Dot Film Festival. IAAH collaborated with <a href="http://www.dreamersinkaesthetics.net/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Mitch Paone</FONT> to tell the story of Katrina and New Orleans an appropriate subject, no less. The piece is complete with quotes from Nagin, the photography of <a href="http://www.leecrum.com" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Lee Crum</FONT> and the Saints marching song.

Dave from <a href="http://www.hellohikimori.com/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Hellohikimori</FONT> tapped IAAH and many others to create original artwork for an upcoming L'oreal show. Both pieces showcased here were produced in print and on-screen at the Paris, France, opening.

An ongoing project, as the editorial process and publishing layers are a bit complex. More Than Music addresses the impact of the Beatles on both society and industry. IAAH had very little to work with asset-wise, as utilizing a photo of the Beatles is illegal. So instead, we interpreted a handful of their songs, along with the iconic image of their Abbey Road album. <a href="http://www.corridorsofmymind.com" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">John Finnell</FONT> remains an integral part of this project.

Truly a celebration of the video game. Game Paused showcases original work that focuses on the broad subject of the video game. These illustrations were inspired by the lust-like quality of the gaming industry. From the standpoint of many people ( IAAH included ), the excitement created by video games is parallel with very few things. Sex could be one.
Another book in progress. This one is based on Bishop Rucyhana's travels through Rwanda, and it aims to capture how his faith handled with the tragedy and horror he witnessed.
Various identities created for an upstart focused on creative writing. The marks focus on the the creative power harnessed by 36 characters.

<a href="http://www.threadless.com/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Threadless</FONT> commissioned IAAH to design a t-shirt based on the phrase, "Let's quietly judge one another." While designing this shirt, we discussed what it felt like to be inappropriately judged, along with the helpless feelings that followed. In many ways, we surmised, false judgments and assumptions can throw a person off-kilter just as effectively as the deeds themselves.

Waist Deep tells a story of a father trying to escape the dark path of drugs and violence in an effort to become a role model for his son. One thing leads to another, and quite the opposite occurs. These comps were developed for <a href="http://www.evoke.la" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Evoke.</FONT>

DC wanted to recreate an old series. The story of Batman and Man-bat. More animalistic in nature and character, he has little regard for human life. This mark was designed to communicate those aspects.

A small piece created for Niko from nitrocorpz. IAAH submitted a sample of favorite tunes for the <a href="http://mixtape.nitrocorpz.com/index.php?mixtape=nessim" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">designer's mixtape</FONT> series.

This one goes back a few years. Ad-One is an agency that specializes in creative recruitment. They needed a unique face in the marketplace, and IAAH created a straightforward mark to give them that. The "+ o" is meant to represent "plus one."
A project commissioned by the well-respected <a href="http://www.polychrome.com/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Polychrome.</FONT> Short timeline. Particular client.
These marks were created for the annual City Stages Festival in 2003-04.
Martian Manhunter is part of the Justice League, and has lived within the Marvel universe for some time. DC wanted to give him his own book, and they tapped us on the shoulder to help brand it.
Hilt is defined as "the maximum extent or degree," and this mark was created for a band by the same name. It was designed to suggest power, energy and upward mobility.
Easy Chair was created for an independent broadcast producer. The client wanted a mark that would reflect that people didn't have to worry; that, indeed, the "easy chair" was reserved for them.

Hellblazer is one of the most successful books in Vertigo's history. With the then-new Constantine film releasing, Vertigo wanted to capitalize on the excitement by redesigning the the original Hellblazer mark. We were tapped with that assignment and, after a lengthy process, created one hell of a mark.
The first title completed for DC. For giggles, have a look at the DC logo. That'll date it immediately.

A mark done in conjunction with <a href="http://www.exopolis.com" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Exopolis</FONT> and the talented <a href="http://www.corridorsofmymind.com" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">John Finnell.</FONT> Created for a new package released by HBO.

Published by Vertigo, Human Target received wide praise. As Wikipedia puts it "The Human Target is an American comic book character created by Len Wei and Carmine Infantino: a unique private detective and bodyguard named Christopher Chance, who operates by impersonating his clients in order to eliminate threats to his principal, hence the idea of becoming a Human Target."
Via Exopolis, the brief tasked us to create a few marks that represented the Sprint brand. You know, talking on the phone.
Accudet is a large-scale real estate developer.
Incines is a film school in South America. Its focus is younger kids, thus the emphasis on children in the mark.

A piece done in collaboration with <a href="www.dreamersinkaesthetics.net/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Mitch Paone</FONT> for a book by the same title. Exploration and subject matter flirted with the realms of experimentation, dreams, and love.

Steve was part of the Digits campaign for TFL (Tobacco Free Living), a Louisiana-based smoking cessation organization. Steve was one of the stronger characters createdŃthe voice talent helped enormously in this spot.

Put a father and daughter in a car, sprinkle in the fact that fingers are playing these roles and that the subject is smoking. Put in the oven to bake, and we'll have a dish of well-deserved, albeit funny, content.
Self-reflection of smoking fingers can be a topic. "You're bigger than this thing, ya gotta beat it."
Shot a few years back and inspired by the frame-ups of Jeunet's work. As you can see, we had some fun on the beach, and the talent was fantastic to work with.

This piece was done in collaboration with two very good friends, <a href="www.evoke.la/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Evan DeHaven</FONT> and <a href="www.laundrymat.tv/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">PJ Richardson.</FONT> The piece was based on music supplied by Funkstorung. DGV was creating a book that featured the visual interpretations of Funkstorung's music.
A piece created for Shift's yearly calander. Created in collaboration with good friend, <a href="www.decoylab.com/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Maiko Kuzunishi.</FONT>
This piece was created for the 2003 Semi-Permanent Conference, and each attendee received a copy of the book. The piece was later picked up by the band Switchfoot for their cover and tour materials.
A triptych created for the online magazine New Piece. Much like the Dedicate piece, I like the aspect of independent pieces that blend to form one united piece.
Computer Arts UK approached IAAH to create a couple of editorial pieces for their magazine: one to show the presence of media in the market and the other for Photoshop tips and tricks.
These pieces are inspired by the music of Funkstorung. DGV was creating a book that featured the visual interpretations of Funkstorung's music.

Dedicate is a wonderful magazine published out of Paris. They tasked IAAH with editorial illustrations to complement a feature based on the subject of SpaceŃor more precisely, "space within space." These illustrations were created to function both independently and together in the form of a triptych.
An illustration commissioned for the Australian clothing label Commune. It was created to communicate a specific message: Stop working for the all mighty dollar!
Two illustrations created for an issue of Audikt, an online showcase by Danny Taobot.

<<a href="http://www.faesthetic.com//" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Faesthetic </FONT>is a creation of the talented <a href="http://www.upso.org/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Dustin Amery Hostetler, aka UPSO.</FONT> This annual book features work by both established and up-and-coming artists, and is designed to allow total creative license on a specific theme.
When people ask what I've been up to, this is what I show them. Various sketches, illustrations, doodles and brain farts over the last few years.
Created photo compositions and illustrations, which were used in a proposal to help land work for the film, The Cave.
A piece for Art Warts, a show in Atlanta. Aspects of graffiti and sex where thrown in a well-seasoned skillet to create this piece.
Various works are thrown into this section. Rhyme, reason and description are left to interpretation.

Quite possibly one of the hardest and most rewarding pieces to date. A wedding invitation that was intended to be self mailing, cost efficient, and out of the norm. We wanted to create something that would be hung up and admired, something that would be a reminder.

Turner South hit the road and documented what was unique about its audience. And, in turn, they learned what "My South" really meant. In conjunction with <a href="http://www.trumpetadvertising.com//" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Trumpet </FONT>, IAAH created a showpiece to showcase the tour. The result: Turner green on sheets of acetate worked very well.

Now, we all know the importance of a well-rounded presentation. Lions Gate Films approached Genex about taking on a site for their upcoming release, Beyond the Sea, which was based on the life of Bobby Darin. In a matter of a couple days and late, late nights, we created a custom cover to house the proposal. Needless to say, the project was awarded to Genex.
In the spirit of the holidays, Genex wanted to send a thank-you gift to friends and family of the agency. Letterpressed coasters were enclosed in an aluminum can, along with a custom music mix.
A custom invitation for Whitney Stewart. Limited production.

These cards were designed to "have legs" from an information standpoint. Knowing that addresses would change, the backs were left blank to remain flexible for all types of information. Handwritten notes were often used.

The One Club of New York invited IAAH to participate in a show focused on political propaganda. In the span of a couple of weeks, <a href="http://www.corridorsofmymind.com//" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">John Finnel </FONT> and I expressed our political frustrations on this poster. As per usual, George W. Bush assisted with a great deal of wonderful content.
A four-panel poster that helped promote the work of Turner South's "My South" tour. This poster detailed the cities visited on the tour.

Brian Collins of Olgivy One / B.I.G spoke at the Birmingham chapter of the AIGA, and questioned, "What is a brand?" And for that matter, "Is Birmingham a brand?" In turn, we designed the front page of the local newspaper to ask this same question.

A series created for Baldwin posters that celebrated the influence of the piano on musical and visual artists. Kobe, Gordon and, of course, Alicia played the piano as children. Others of us weren't so lucky.
A poster created to help remind people that there is a community for them to join. It's run by designer's, it can be exceptional.
Part of a book project. This limited edition, one-of-a-kind piece was comprised of Plexiglass and acetate for a see-through experience. A modified Nike shoe box served as the slip case. Just do it.

Created to promote and celebrate the civil rights trail. The Ride to Freedom was intended to drum up interest from travel writers around the country.The invitation pictured here was adhered to the inside of a 24 X 36 poster. Photos: coming soon.

Part of the Redesign / Reconstruct project created by <a href="http://www.plinko.com/home.php/" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Plinko. </FONT> The project involves choosing original artwork and reconstructing, reinterpreting and redesigning it.

Many agencies received an RFP for the Corpse Bride site. But only one was lucky. These comprehensives were done for <a href="http://www.exopolis.com//" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Exopolis.</FONT> The design was meant to work in a negative, positive manner, up or down, depending on the section chosen.

I Must Create was the effort of myself, Bryant Fernandez, Jared Lyvers and Matt Cross. As with all good collaborations, each of us were essential to the process. And the response to it was humbling. We stumped a lot of people with the Pantone chips. Visit the site<a href="http://www.imustcreate.com//" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003"> here.</FONT>
This site was designed and launched with a very quick turnaround. 3 weeks, from start to finish. Evan did an amazing job animating it, and Warner Bros. was easy going, from a client standpoint.

This deep and involved site featured a large, interwoven experience with a good amount of film information. It lives <a href="http://www.imustcreate.com//" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">here</FONT> for the time being. Have a look.
A series of comps that were created to establish the look and feel of the Life Aquatic website. Created for <a href="http://www.65media.com//" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">65 Media.</FONT>

Genex was awarded the Constantine job, and it sat dormant for months while the film's release date was continually pushed back. In the meantime, these boards were created to set the look-and-feel of the upcoming site.
For TCM's Summer Under the Star's series, this online piece features an old rotoscope, which allows you to choose your favorite character and find a listing for their next TCM showing.

<<a href="http://www.arterycreative.com//" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Artery</FONT> is a creative consultancy in Boston. IAAH and <a href="http://www.evoke.la//" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Evoke</FONT> were approached by Artery to give their site a flexible web presence, so they could change the look-and-feel on the fly.

Site developed for Warner Brothers and <a href="http://www.genex.com//" target="_blank"><FONT COLOR="#fec003">Genex.</FONT> Mystic river is a character driven piece that examined the six main characters and their relationship to one another. A great film if you haven't seen it.
A luscious site for a sensuous and beautiful film. This site is a rather simple EPK (electronic press kit), and its design focused on the movie's sublime photography as a backdrop.
